page: Amazon Web Services c6i.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the Intel Xeon 8375C processor. This x86_64 architecture server features 16 virtual CPUs mapped to 8 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a clock speed of 3.5 GHz. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. The processor includes a 54 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 for vector operations and SHA-NI for cryptographic acceleration. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 6 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations place the c6i.4xlarge in the middle tier of performance. In CPU-intensive tasks, the instance achieves a stress-ng single-core score of 1892.29 and a multi-core score of 15472.98, alongside a Geekbench multi-core score of 9680.0. Memory performance is characterized by a cached read-only bandwidth of 828.26 GB/sec, which drops to 95.59 GB/sec for large uncached blocks. Cryptographic capabilities are supported by hardware acceleration, yielding a Passmark CPU encryption test score of 14558.89. For specialized workloads, the server processes 15.63 million Redis SET operations per second and handles static web serving at a throughput of 22.41 GB/sec. Small-scale language model inference on a 135M parameter model reaches 223.63 tokens per second for text generation, though performance drops to 11.46 tokens per second on a 7B model.

Given its compute-to-memory ratio of 1:2, the c6i.4xlarge offers a balanced profile for compute-bound workloads that do not require massive memory footprints or local storage. The absence of bundled local NVMe storage means storage-heavy databases will rely on network-attached storage, which may introduce latency tradeoffs. This instance is highly suited for high-performance web serving, distributed analytics, batch processing, software compilation, and moderate-scale scientific modeling.
description: Amazon Web Services c6i.4xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz. It features 16 vCPUs, 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory, and a baseline network bandwidth of 6 Gbps, with no local storage or GPU accelerators. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance delivers balanced mid-range performance, highlighted by a Geekbench multi-core score of 9680.0 and a static web serving throughput of 22.41 GB/sec. It also processes 15.63 million Redis SET operations per second. With a 2.0 GB RAM-to-vCPU ratio, this instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for compute-heavy workloads that do not require local storage, making it well-suited for software compilation, batch processing, and high-throughput web serving.
